claim: Mixture of Experts architectures with high sparsity (few active parameters per token) enable competitive performance at lower inference cost and faster generation
actionable: Use sparse MoE models like Qwen 3 Next for production deployments when inference speed and cost matter more than peak accuracy

claim: Multi-token prediction training (predicting multiple tokens simultaneously) improves both inference efficiency and model quality
actionable: When fine-tuning or evaluating models, prefer ones trained with multi-token objectives over pure next-token prediction

claim: Models benefit from hybrid attention mechanisms (not standard transformers) but proprietary labs keep this hidden in closed-source models
actionable: Experiment with custom attention mechanisms in your own models; proprietary models likely already do this

claim: Training on less data (15T vs 36T tokens) with better filtering and data curation can match or exceed models trained on double the tokens
actionable: Focus on data quality and filtering over raw corpus size when training models; better curation compounds in improvement

claim: Models tend to think in English even when multilingual, using English for reasoning then switching to target language for output
actionable: When prompting multilingual models for complex tasks, expect reasoning in English; target language output is still accurate
